trad 1: I have learned the whole art of leave-taking
trad 2: I have studied the science of parting
trad 3: I made myself an expert in farewells

trad 1: In bare-headed night-lamentations.
trad 2: in the laments of the night, when a woman’s hair flows loose.
trad 3: By studying laments, the night-fall of a woman’s hair. / Oxen chew their cud;

trad 1: The oxen chew: anticipation lingers
trad 2: The oxen chew, and expectation lingers –
trad 3: anticipation lags;

trad 2: the last hour of the town’s vigil;
trad 3: It is the town’s last restless hour;

Verso Ruso (Original): И чту обряд той петушиной ночи,
trad 1: And I honour the ceremony of that cock-crying night
trad 2: and I revere the rites of that night when cocks were crowing,
trad 3: And I praise that ritual night when the cocks crowed

trad 1: When raising their burden of sorrow for a departing traveller
trad 2: and when eyes red with crying, lifting their load of itinerant sorrow,
trad 3: And eyelids, heavy with the griefs that pass,

trad 1: Eyes that were res with weeping gazed into the distance
trad 2: gazed into the distance,
trad 3: Opened to the light, while her weeping flowed

Verso Ruso (Original): И женский плач мешался с пеньем муз.
trad 1: And the wailing of women mingled with the song of the muses.
trad 2: and a woman’s weeping mingled with the Muses singing.
trad 3: Into the sound of the Muses singing.

Verso Ruso (Original): Кто может знать при слове - расставанье,
trad 1: In that word “leave-taking” who can tell
trad 2: Who knows, when the word ‘parting’ is spoken,
trad 3: Who knows, when the time comes to say goodbye,

trad 1: What kind of separation is in store for us,
trad 2: what kind of separation lies in store for us,
trad 3: What separation we are meant to bear

trad 1: What it is the crying of cock promises
trad 2: what the cock’s crowing augurs for us
trad 3: And what for us cockcrow shall signify

trad 1: When fire burns on the acropolis:
trad 2: when the fire burns on the acropolis,
trad 3: When the acropolis burns like a flare,

Verso Ruso (Original): И на заре какой-то новой жизни,
trad 1: And in the first red dawn of some new life
trad 2: and why at the dawn of some new life,
trad 3: And why, at the new daybreak of a life.

Verso Ruso (Original): Когда в сенях лениво вол жует,
trad 1: When lazily the ox is chewing in the shade,
trad 2: when the ox is chewing lazily in the shed,
trad 3: When the ox is ruminating in his stall,

trad 1: Why does the cock, the new life’s own town-crier
trad 3: The herald cock, prophetic of rebirth.

trad 1: On the city wall beat madly with his wings?
trad 2: the cock, herald of the new life, flaps its wings on the town wall?
trad 3: Should flap his wings on the town wall?

trad 1: I love the way the thread is spun –
trad 2: I like the routine of spinning:
trad 3: I bless the craft of spinning: the to-and-fro

trad 1: The shuttle runs to and from, the spindle hums –
trad 2: the shuttle moves to and fro, the spindle hums.
trad 3: Action of the shuttle, the way the spindle hums.

trad 1: Look now – already like swansdown
trad 2: Look, barefooted Delia, like swansdown,
trad 3: Look I barefooted Delia, light as a feather.

trad 1: Barefooted Delia flies to meet you!
trad 2: is already flying to meet you.
trad 3: Hurries to meet you, flying as she comes. / Oh, how scrawny is the language of joy.

trad 1: O the meagre pattern of our life –
trad 2: Oh, how scanty is the language of joy,
trad 3: That weak foundation of our mortal lot!

trad 1: Even our happiest words are threadbare!
trad 2: that meagre foundation of our life
trad 3: Everything happened before;

Verso Ruso (Original): Всё было встарь. Всё повторится снова.
trad 1: Everything has been of old and will be again:
trad 2: All was before, all will be repeated again,
trad 3: it will happen again.

Verso Ruso (Original): И сладок нам лишь узнаванья миг.
trad 1: For us, only the moment of recognition is sweet.
trad 2: and only the moment of recognition brings us delight.
trad 3: Only the flash of recognition brings delight.

trad 1: So let it be: the little gleaming image
trad 2: So let it be: a small transparent figures lies,
trad 3: Be it so: a small transparent puppet lies,

trad 1: Lies on the spotless earthen dish.
trad 2: like a stretched out squirrel-skin,

trad 1: Like the stretched-out skin of a squirrel
trad 2: on a clean earthenware dish;
trad 3: Like a dried squirrel skin / Extended on a plate.

trad 1: Stooping over the wax the young girl gazes.
trad 2: a girl is gazing, bending down over the wax. / It is not for us to tell fortunes about the
trad 3: While a girl crouches, staring, over the image.

Verso Ruso (Original): Не нам гадать о греческом Эребе,
trad 1: It is not for us to guess about Grecian Erebus:
trad 2: Greek Erebus;

Verso Ruso (Original): Для женщин воск - что для мужчины медь,
trad 1: What´s bronze form man for woman is only wax.
trad 2: wax is for women what bronze is for men.
trad 3: Wax is for women what bronze is for men. / We, who cannot prophesy of Erebus,

Verso Ruso (Original): Нам только в битвах выпадает жребий,
trad 1: Our destiny befalls us only in battles –
trad 2: It is only in battle that the lot falls upon us;
trad 3: Only in battle dare confront our fate —

trad 1: They see the future as they die.
trad 2: but to them it is given to die while telling fortunes.
trad 3: But their gift is to die while telling fortunes.
